reply:
[[BLOCK:slide33_shape1]]
AICc (corrected) imposes a greater penalty for additional parameters.  
AICc = AIC + (2K(K+1)/(n-k-1)), where n = sample size, and k = number of parameters.  
Burnham & Anderson (2002) recommend using AICc, especially when n is small and k is large.  
As n increases, AICc converges to AIC.  
AICc should be used regardless of the size of n and k.
